get.summary:
GetRunCancellation is the worker's cancellation-polling RPC.
Workers SHOULD call this periodically during execution and
gracefully terminate the agent when `cancelled = true`.

post.summary:
PostWorkerEvent is the worker's push channel: each SDK message
(or worker-emitted lifecycle signal) becomes a StreamEvent and
is persisted + broadcast to streaming subscribers.

CompleteRun is the worker's success-terminal signal. Includes
the Nightshift session_id and the RunUsage summary from the
agent SDK's terminal message.
FailRun is the worker's failure-terminal signal.

get.summary: ListRuns returns runs matching the supplied filters.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Optional filter: only return runs owned by this user.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Optional filter: only return runs with this status.

 - RUN_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: Proto3 default. Conforming implementations should not return this.
 - RUN_STATUS_PENDING: The run record has been created but execution has not begun.
Implementations transition runs from PENDING to RUNNING once the
underlying workload (for example a Kubernetes Job) has started.
 - RUN_STATUS_RUNNING: The run is executing. Events are being posted through the worker
callback surface and forwarded to streaming subscribers.
 - RUN_STATUS_COMPLETED: The run finished successfully. `session_id` and `usage` are
populated on the Run record.
 - RUN_STATUS_ERROR: The run finished unsuccessfully. `error` is populated with an
implementation-supplied message.
 - RUN_STATUS_INTERRUPTED: The run was cancelled before natural completion, either by an
explicit `InterruptRun` call or by an implementation-side
timeout / deadline. `error` MAY carry a reason.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Optional filter: only return runs for this session_id.

parameters.name: invokerType
parameters.description:
Optional filter: only return runs invoked by this (type, id)
pair. Setting `invoker_type` without `invoker_id` filters on
type alone.

 - INVOKER_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Proto3 default. Conforming implementations should not return this.
 - INVOKER_TYPE_USER: A human user created the run directly. `invoker_id` is the
user's identifier.
 - INVOKER_TYPE_SCHEDULE: A schedule (see chunk 7 Scheduling) fired and created the run on
the owning user's behalf. `invoker_id` is the schedule
identifier.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of runs to return. The server may cap the value.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Implementation-defined order specification. Conventional format
is `"<field> <asc|desc>"`, for example `"created_at desc"`.

post.summary:
CreateRun creates a run record, reserves a session identifier,
and launches the underlying run workload.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CreateRunRequest is the request message for CreateRun.

delete.summary:
DeleteSession removes every run with the given session_id and
the underlying SDK session state. Irreversible.

get.summary: GetRun returns a single run's metadata.

ListRunEvents returns a paginated history of events for a run
without streaming. Useful for offline inspection and tests.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Maximum number of events to return. The server may cap the
value.

get.summary:
StreamRunEvents is a server-streaming RPC that delivers every
event from a run. Defaults to replaying all persisted events
followed by live events until the run terminates.

parameters.name: includeHistory
parameters.description:
When true (the default effective behavior for new subscribers),
the server replays all persisted events for the run before
streaming live events. When false, only events produced after
the subscription MAY be delivered. Implementations that cannot
selectively suppress history SHOULD accept the field and ignore
it.

parameters.name: fromIndex
parameters.description:
Optional lower-bound index filter. When non-zero, only events
with `index >= from_index` are delivered.

post.summary:
InterruptRun signals cancellation. The run reaches
RUN_STATUS_INTERRUPTED asynchronously; callers observe the
transition via StreamRunEvents or a subsequent GetRun.

CreateSession mints a fresh session identifier without launching
a run.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CreateSessionRequest is the request message for CreateSession.

sessionId.description:
Nightshift session identifier the worker was running under. The
implementation persists this on the Run so the next run
continuing the conversation can reference it.

usage.description:
Cost + token usage captured from the agent SDK's terminal
message.

WorkersCompleteRunBody.description:
CompleteRunRequest is the request message for CompleteRun. Called
by the worker when the agent finishes successfully.

error.description: Free-form error message. Preserved on the Run.

WorkersFailRunBody.description:
FailRunRequest is the request message for FailRun. Called by the
worker when the agent fails to complete.

WorkersInterruptRunBody.description: InterruptRunRequest is the request message for InterruptRun.

event.description:
The event being posted. Implementations MUST assign a monotonic
`index` server-side; the value on the incoming event is ignored.

WorkersPostWorkerEventBody.description:
PostWorkerEventRequest is the request message for PostWorkerEvent.
Called by the run workload (for example a Kubernetes Job pod) to
push a single event back to the control plane.

protobufNullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.

v1CompleteRunResponse.description:
CompleteRunResponse is the response message for CompleteRun. It is
intentionally empty — the terminal state is visible on the Run via
GetRun.

sessionId.description:
Optional Nightshift session id to continue an existing multi-
turn conversation. Empty means start a new session; the created
run's `session_id` will be assigned by the implementation at
completion time.

userId.description:
Owning user. Implementations typically derive this from the
caller's auth principal; it is carried explicitly on the request
so internal callers (for example a Scheduling service firing a
cron) can attribute runs to the owning user.

invokerType.description: Categorization of who is creating this run.

invokerId.description: Identifier of the triggering entity, per InvokerType semantics.

v1CreateRunRequest.description: CreateRunRequest is the request message for CreateRun.

run.description:
The newly created run. Typically in RUN_STATUS_PENDING; may be
RUN_STATUS_RUNNING already if the implementation's launch path
is synchronous.

v1CreateRunResponse.description: CreateRunResponse is the response message for CreateRun.

v1CreateSessionRequest.description: CreateSessionRequest is the request message for CreateSession.

v1CreateSessionResponse.description: CreateSessionResponse is the response message for CreateSession.

v1DeleteSessionResponse.description: DeleteSessionResponse is the response message for DeleteSession.

v1FailRunResponse.description:
FailRunResponse is the response message for FailRun. It is
intentionally empty.

cancelled.description:
Whether the control plane has requested cancellation of this
run. Once true, the worker should invoke its SDK's interrupt
path, drain in-flight events, and call CompleteRun or FailRun as
appropriate.

v1GetRunCancellationResponse.description:
GetRunCancellationResponse is the response message for
GetRunCancellation.

v1GetRunResponse.description: GetRunResponse is the response message for GetRun.

run.description: The run after the interrupt signal has been recorded.

v1InterruptRunResponse.description:
InterruptRunResponse is the response message for InterruptRun. The
returned Run reflects the post-interrupt state; for asynchronous
implementations the status may still be RUN_STATUS_RUNNING until
the worker observes the cancellation signal — callers observe the
transition to RUN_STATUS_INTERRUPTED via the event stream or a
subsequent GetRun.

v1InvokerType.description:
InvokerType categorizes who or what triggered a run. It is load-
bearing for billing attribution, audit, and rate limiting.

 - INVOKER_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Proto3 default. Conforming implementations should not return this.
 - INVOKER_TYPE_USER: A human user created the run directly. `invoker_id` is the
user's identifier.
 - INVOKER_TYPE_SCHEDULE: A schedule (see chunk 7 Scheduling) fired and created the run on
the owning user's behalf. `invoker_id` is the schedule
identifier.

events.description: Matching events on this page, ordered by ascending index.

nextPageToken.description:
Opaque pagination token. Empty when there are no further
results.

v1ListRunEventsResponse.description: ListRunEventsResponse is the response message for ListRunEvents.

nextPageToken.description:
Opaque pagination token. Empty when there are no further
results.

v1ListRunsResponse.description: ListRunsResponse is the response message for ListRuns.

index.description: The server-assigned index the event was persisted at.

v1PostWorkerEventResponse.description: PostWorkerEventResponse is the response message for PostWorkerEvent.

id.description: Server-assigned identifier. Stable for the lifetime of the run.

prompt.description:
The task instruction the agent was launched with. Preserved
verbatim so runs are reproducible and auditable.

sessionId.description:
Nightshift-owned session identifier, used to resume a multi-turn
conversation across runs. Implementations map this to whatever
session identifier the agent SDK uses internally; the spec does
NOT expose the SDK session id. Empty until the first completed
run sets it (or until `CreateRun` is called with an explicit
`session_id` to continue an existing conversation).

userId.description:
Owning user. Credentials, artifacts, per-user state (see chunk 8)
are all scoped to this value.

invokerType.description: Categorization of who triggered this run.

invokerId.description:
Identifier of the triggering entity, interpretation depends on
invoker_type. For INVOKER_TYPE_USER this is a user id; for
INVOKER_TYPE_SCHEDULE this is a schedule id.

error.description:
Populated with an implementation-supplied message when status is
RUN_STATUS_ERROR or RUN_STATUS_INTERRUPTED.

eventCount.description:
Number of events persisted for this run. Grows monotonically
during RUNNING and is frozen at a terminal status.

usage.description:
Aggregate resource usage captured at completion. Zero-valued
until a run reaches RUN_STATUS_COMPLETED.

createdAt.description: Time the run record was created.

startedAt.description:
Time the run transitioned from PENDING to RUNNING. Zero value if
the run never started.

endedAt.description:
Time the run reached a terminal status. Zero value while the run
is PENDING or RUNNING.

v1Run.description: Run is the metadata record for a single agent execution.

v1RunStatus.description:
RunStatus is the lifecycle state of a Run.

 - RUN_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: Proto3 default. Conforming implementations should not return this.
 - RUN_STATUS_PENDING: The run record has been created but execution has not begun.
Implementations transition runs from PENDING to RUNNING once the
underlying workload (for example a Kubernetes Job) has started.
 - RUN_STATUS_RUNNING: The run is executing. Events are being posted through the worker
callback surface and forwarded to streaming subscribers.
 - RUN_STATUS_COMPLETED: The run finished successfully. `session_id` and `usage` are
populated on the Run record.
 - RUN_STATUS_ERROR: The run finished unsuccessfully. `error` is populated with an
implementation-supplied message.
 - RUN_STATUS_INTERRUPTED: The run was cancelled before natural completion, either by an
explicit `InterruptRun` call or by an implementation-side
timeout / deadline. `error` MAY carry a reason.

totalCostUsd.description:
Total cost in USD, as reported by the SDK's result payload
(pre-markup).

inputTokens.description: Total input tokens billed across all turns in this run.

outputTokens.description: Total output tokens billed across all turns in this run.

cacheReadTokens.description: Cache-read tokens (prompt prefixes served from prompt cache).

cacheCreationTokens.description:
Cache-creation tokens (prompt prefixes written into prompt
cache).

v1RunUsage.description:
RunUsage is the resource-usage summary captured at run completion
from the agent SDK's terminal message.

index.description:
Monotonic index within the run, starting at 0. The (run_id,
index) pair is unique.

type.description:
* `"system.worker_started"` — emitted by the worker at run
    start.
  * `"user"`, `"assistant"` — SDK message kinds.
  * `"result.<subtype>"`, `"system.<subtype>"` — SDK
    intermediate signals.
  * `"rate_limit_event"` — SDK rate-limit notification.
  * `"artifact.created"`, `"artifact.updated"`,
    `"artifact.app_deployed"` — forwarded from the Artifacts
    interface (see `artifacts.proto`, `ArtifactEventType`).

Conforming implementations SHOULD use these well-known strings
when the payload matches their shape, and MAY introduce
additional string values. Subscribers MUST treat unknown `type`
values as opaque and preserve them when replaying history.
type.title:
Discriminator describing the payload shape. Well-known values
include:

timestamp.description: Wallclock time the event was produced.

raw.description:
Opaque structured payload. Schema varies by `type`. Conforming
implementations MUST preserve the payload bit-for-bit through
the streaming and history-replay paths.

v1StreamEvent.description:
StreamEvent is a single message in a run's event stream. Events
carry arbitrary SDK output, worker-emitted lifecycle signals, and
forwarded artifact events.

event.description:
One event from the run. The stream completes (no error) once the
run reaches a terminal status and all buffered events have been
delivered.

v1StreamRunEventsResponse.description:
StreamRunEventsResponse is a single message on the StreamRunEvents
server stream.
